title: Dengue Hemorrhagic Fever — Warning Signs Progression

patient_data.chief_complaint: High fever for 4 days with worsening abdominal pain and bleeding gums
symptoms: High fever (40.1°C) for 4 days — now defervescing
Abdominal pain — right upper quadrant tenderness
patient_data.history: Lives in tropical area (Southeast Asia), mosquito exposure. Neighbor hospitalized with dengue last week.

should_detect: Critical phase transition: defervescence with warning signs
Thrombocytopenia with bleeding — monitor for DIC
DO NOT give NSAIDs or aspirin — increases bleeding risk
IV fluid resuscitation — careful to avoid fluid overload
Monitor for dengue shock syndrome progression
Serial hematocrit and platelet monitoring every 4-6 hours
